revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:šļĒśĘĚŚ§©Á•ěÁ§ĺ (šļ¨ťÉĹŚłā)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = GojŇć TenjingŇę (šļĒśĘĚŚ§©Á•ěŚģģ) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:2013 1105 Gojoten Jinja Shrine torii.jpg|250px|Photo taken November 5, 2013]]
East gate and torii (Nishinotoin Street) | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto|en|ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1077022|lt=ShimogyŇć-ku|1=ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto}}, Nishinotoin-nishiiru, Tenjinmae 351, Matsubara Street | Latitude = 34 | Latitude minutes = 59 | Latitude seconds = 55.2 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude minutes = 45 | Longitude seconds = 17.4 | E/W = E | Map code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto city2 | deity = {{ill|Sukuna Hikona|ja|ŚįĎŚĹ¶ŚźćŚĎĹ|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukuna Hikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to „āĻ„āĮ„Éä„Éď„ā≥„Éä}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|śĚĎÁ§ĺ|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ŤŅĎšĽ£Á§ĺś†ľŚą∂Śļ¶|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Traditionally in the {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=13th year of Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (794 CE) | Main hall = | Other names = GojŇć Tenjinsha, Tenshi no Miya, Tenshi-sha | Festivals = May 10 | Rituals = }} [[File:2013 1105 Gojoten Jinja Shrine Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine building and plaque]] '''GojŇć TenjingŇę''' (šļĒśĘĚŚ§©Á•ěŚģģ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto|en|ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1077022|lt=ShimogyŇć-ku|1=ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City. Its former shrine Rank is {{ill|Village shrine|ja|śĚĎÁ§ĺ|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ŤŅĎšĽ£Á§ĺś†ľŚą∂Śļ¶|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The shrine is venerated for its blessings in warding off illness, exorcising misfortune, promoting agriculture, and medicine.[[#KyotoHistory|Kyoto Historical Dictionary]] pp. 101-102 The registered name of its religious corporation is ''GojŇć Tenjinsha,'' and it is colloquially known as '''Tenshi no Miya''' (Heavenly Angel Shrine) or '''Tenshi-sha''' (Angel Shrine).{{Cite web|url=https://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=1&ManageCode=1000069|title=GojŇć TenjingŇę (Tenshi no Miya)|work=Kyoto Tourism Navi|publisher=Kyoto City Tourism and MICE Promotion Office|accessdate=2015-12-07}}[[#KyotoShrineDictionary|Kyoto and Yamashiro Shrine Encyclopedia]] pp. 260-262 The shrine's name, ''Tenjin'' (Ś§©Á•ě), means ''heavenly deity'' and has no connection to the ''Tenjin'' (Sugawara no Michizane) associated with academic pursuits. == Enshrined Deities == * Primary deity: {{ill|Sukuna Hikona|ja|ŚįĎŚĹ¶ŚźćŚĎĹ|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukuna Hikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to „āĻ„āĮ„Éä„Éď„ā≥„Éä}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) * Additional deities: {{ill|ŇĆkuninushi|en|ŇĆkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=ŇĆkuninushi|1=ŇĆkuninushi}} (ŇĆnamuchi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu ŇĆmikami|1=Amaterasu}} (Amaterasu ŇĆmikami) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=13th year of Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (794 CE) during the {{ill|Heian-kyŇć|en|Heian-kyŇć|qid=Q751907|lt=transfer of the capital to Heian-kyŇć|1=Heian-kyŇć}} under {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}. {{ill|KŇękai|en|KŇękai|qid=Q222227|lt=KŇękai|1=KŇękai}} enshrined a portion of the heavenly deities (''Amatsukami'') from {{ill|Uda District, Nara|en|Uda District, Nara|qid=Q1131315|lt=Uda District|1=Uda District, Nara}}, Yamato Province, as part of the establishment. It is regarded as the oldest shrine within the {{ill|Kyoto inner city|ja|śīõšł≠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto inner city|1=Central Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q14221649}}. The shrine gained prominence for prayers to ward off illness and epidemics. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, it became revered as a deity of medicine and epidemic prevention. Both {{ill|SaichŇć|en|SaichŇć|qid=Q449943|lt=SaichŇć|1=SaichŇć}} and KŇękai reportedly prayed at this shrine for safe passage before departing for {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|qid=Q9683|lt=Tang China|1=Tang dynasty}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it was known as ''Tenshi no Miya'' (Heavenly Angel Shrine). The name ''GojŇć TenjingŇę'' was adopted during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|qid=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}}.The Kyoto City historical plaque at the eastern gate. The term ''GojŇć'' originates from the nearby {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|śĚĺŚéüťÄö|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17157800}}, formerly known as GojŇć Street. The shrine was destroyed by fires multiple times, including during the {{ill|ŇĆnin War|en|ŇĆnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=ŇĆnin War|1=ŇĆnin War}}, the {{ill|Great Tenmei Fire|ja|Ś§©śėé„ĀģŚ§ßÁĀę|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tenmei Fire|1=Tenmei Fire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442674}} of 1788, and the {{ill|Dondon Yake|ja|„Ā©„āď„Ā©„āďÁĄľ„ĀĎ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dondon Yake|1=Dondon-yake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11273958}} in 1864. Each time, it was rebuilt. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was classified as a ''Village Shrine.'' == Festivals == The shrine hosts various festivals, attracting worshippers seeking blessings for health and misfortune removal. During {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|qid=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}}, the shrine distributes ''treasure ship'' (''takarabune'') illustrations, believed to bring good fortune. * New Year's Festival (''Saitan-sai''): January 1 * Setsubun Festival: February 3 * festival: May 10 * Parishioner Festival: October (on Health and Sports Day) == Precincts == [[File:2013 1105 Gojoten Jinja Shrine Shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine hall]] * Main hall: Rebuilt after the {{ill|Dondon Yake|ja|„Ā©„āď„Ā©„āďÁĄľ„ĀĎ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dondon Yake|1=Dondon-yake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11273958}} in 1864. * Worship hall * Shrine office * North gate * East gate (main entrance) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Chikushi {{ill|TenmangŇę|en|TenmangŇę|qid=Q798838|lt=TenmangŇę Shrine|1=TenmangŇę}} * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine|en|Sarutahiko Shrine|qid=Q11572141|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|1=Sarutahiko Shrine}} * Benzaiten Shrine * GŇćshi Shrine: Includes ShiratayŇę Shrine, ŇĆkuninushi Shrine, Konpira Shrine, and Fukube Shrine. File:2016 0620 Kyoto Gojo Tenjinsha Nouth Gate.jpg|North gate and torii (Matsubara Street) File:2016 0620 Kyoto Gojo Tenjinsha Keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in the precincts File:2016 0620 Kyoto Gojo Tenjinsha Tenmangu.jpg|Chikushi TenmangŇę Shrine == Surrounding Area == === Tenshi-tsukinuke === The surrounding area, known as ''Tenshi-tsukinuke,'' received its name during the {{ill|TenshŇć (Momoyama period)|en|TenshŇć (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=TenshŇć Era|1=TenshŇć (Momoyama period)}} (1586), when {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} expanded Kyoto's road network. Roads were cut through the shrine's sacred grove.[http://j-town.net/kyoto/news/localtv/193786.html?p=all Kyoto‚Äôs Mysterious Place names: Tenshi-tsukinuke] - J-Town Net Kyoto Prefecture, October 26, 2014 File:2016 0620 Kyoto Tenshi-tsukinuke Plate.jpg|Street sign for Tenshi-tsukinuke Second District == Access == === By Train === * {{ill|Karasuma Line|en|Karasuma Line|qid=Q762347|lt=Kyoto Municipal Subway Karasuma Line|1=Karasuma Line}} ''' {{ill|GojŇć Station|en|GojŇć Station|qid=Q4980394|lt=GojŇć Station (Kyoto)|1=GojŇć Station}} ‚Äď 700 m northwest ''' {{ill|ShijŇć Station|en|ShijŇć Station|qid=Q2328455|lt=ShijŇć Station (Kyoto)|1=ShijŇć Station}} ‚Äď 700 m southwest * {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|en|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|qid=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} ''' {{ill|Karasuma Station|en|Karasuma Station|qid=Q3969852|lt=Karasuma Station|1=Karasuma Station}} ‚Äď 800 m southwest === By Bus === * {{ill|Kyoto City Bus|ja|šļ¨ťÉĹŚłāŚĖ∂„Éź„āĻ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Bus|1=Kyoto City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4858667}}: Get off at "Nishinotoin Matsubara." == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Heibonsha Regional Reference Center|date=1997-02-19|title=Kyoto and Yamashiro Shrine Encyclopedia|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=9784582134018|ref=KyotoShrineDictionary}} * {{Cite book|editor=Heibonsha Regional Reference Center|date=1979-09-20|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Vol. 27 Kyoto City names|publisher=Heibonsha|ref=27KyotoCitynames}} * {{Cite book|author=Ishida Takayoshi|date=1994-11-15|title=Kyoto Historical Dictionary|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Oraisha|isbn=440402133 X|ref=KyotoHistory}} == See Also == * {{ill|GojŇć Tenjin Shrine (Tokyo)|ja|šļĒśĘĚŚ§©Á•ěÁ§ĺ (ŚŹįśĚĪŚĆļ)|1=Gojo Tenjin Shrine (Tokyo)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GojŇć Tenjin Shrine (Tokyo)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojo Tenjin Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} ‚Äď A branch shrine in Tokyo. * {{ill|Yakusojin|ja|ŤĖ¨Á•ĖÁ•ě|1=Three Medicine Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Medicine Gods|qq=}} * {{ill|Gikeiki|en|Gikeiki|qid=Q2600904|lt=Gikeiki|1=Gikeiki}} == External Links == * [https://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=1&ManageCode=1000069 GojŇć TenjingŇę (Tenshi no Miya)] - Kyoto Tourism Navi {{Shinto-stub}} {{ShintoSidebox}} {{Rakuya Tenmangu 25 Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = GojŇć Tenjin-gŇę | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:2013 pp05 Gojoten Jinja Shrine torii.jpg|250px|Photographed on November 5, 2013]]
East Gate and Torii (Nishinotoin-dori) | location = {{ill|ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto|en|ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto|1=ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|59|55.2|N|135|45|17.4|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) | FormerRank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|śĚĎÁ§ĺ|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ŤŅĎšĽ£Á§ĺś†ľŚą∂Śļ¶|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Traditional date: {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = {{ill|GojŇć Tenjinsha|ja|šļĒśĚ°Ś§©Á•ěÁ§ĺ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GojŇć Tenjinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to šļĒśĘĚŚ§©Á•ěÁ§ĺ|1=GojŇć Tenjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372505}}, {{ill|Tenshi no Miya|ja|Ś§©šĹŅ„ĀģŚģģ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshi no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504341|1=Tenshi no Miya}}, {{ill|Tenshi-sha|ja|Ś§©šĹŅÁ§ĺ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshi-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504343|1=Tenshi-sha}} | Festivals = May 10 | Website = }} [[File:2013 pp05 Gojoten Jinja Shrine Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine hall and plaque]] '''GojŇć Tenjin-gŇę''' (šļĒśĘĚŚ§©Á•ěŚģģ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto|en|ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyo-ku|1=ShimogyŇć-ku, Kyoto}} district of Kyoto City. It is a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|śĚĎÁ§ĺ|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ŤŅĎšĽ£Á§ĺś†ľŚą∂Śļ¶|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} known for worshipping deities associated with disease prevention, exorcism, agriculture, and medicine.[[#KyotoHistoricalSites|Kyoto Historical Sites Encyclopedia]] pp. 101‚Äď102 The registered religious corporation name is "GojŇć Tenjinsha." Locally, it is also called "Tenshi no Miya" or "Tenshi-sha."{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=1&ManageCode=1000069|title=šļĒśĘĚŚ§©Á•ěŚģģÔľąŚ§©šĹŅ„ĀģŚģģÔľČ|work=Kyoto Tourism Navi|publisher=Kyoto City Tourism and MICE Promotion Office|access-date=2015-12-07}}[[#KyotoShrineEncyclopedia|Kyoto Shrine Encyclopedia]] pp. 260‚Äď262 The shrine's name "Tenjin" („ÉÜ„É≥„ā∑„É≥) refers to "Amatsukami" (heavenly deities) and is unrelated to the deification of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=ŤŹÖŚéüťĀďÁúü|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=ŤŹÖŚéüťĀďÁúü|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}, commonly referred to as Tenjin („ÉÜ„É≥„āł„É≥). == Enshrined Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) * Secondary deities: {{ill|ŇĆkuninushi|en|ŇĆkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=ŇĆkuninushi|1=ŇĆkuninushi}} (ŇĆnamuji no Mikoto), {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu ŇĆmikami|1=Amaterasu}} (Amaterasu ŇĆmikami) == History == According to shrine legend, {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} ordered the shrine's establishment in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794) during the relocation of the capital to {{ill|Heian-kyŇć|en|Heian-kyŇć|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyŇć|1=Heian-kyŇć}}. {{ill|KŇękai|en|KŇękai|qid=Q222227|lt=KŇękai|1=KŇękai}} enshrined "Amatsukami" (heavenly deities) from {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}'s Uda District. The shrine is considered the oldest in central Kyoto. Originally called "Tenshi no Miya" or "Tenshi-sha," the shrine was often sought for prayers against illness and epidemics. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, it gained recognition as a shrine for epidemic prevention and as a deity of medicine. Historical accounts state that both {{ill|SaichŇć|en|SaichŇć|qid=Q449943|lt=SaichŇć|1=SaichŇć}} and KŇękai prayed for safe returns from Tang China at this shrine. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, it became a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}, as recorded in the "Gion Shrine Record." Later, Emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|qid=Q316658|lt=Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}} renamed the shrine "GojŇć Tenjin-gŇę."The shrine‚Äôs history plaque at the East Gate, installed by Kyoto City. The name "GojŇć" is derived from the former name of {{ill|Matsubara Street|ja|śĚĺŚéüťÄö|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street|1=Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17157800}}, which was once called GojŇć Street. The shrine has been reconstructed multiple times after being destroyed by fires, including the {{ill|Tenmei Fire|ja|Ś§©śėé„ĀģŚ§ßÁĀę|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei Fire|qq=}} in 1788 and the {{ill|Kinmon incident|en|Kinmon incident|qid=Q139871|lt=Kinmon Incident|1=Kinmon incident}} in 1864. == Festivals == The shrine attracts many visitors during its festivals, particularly for exorcism and healing. During {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|qid=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}}, "treasure ship" talismans, believed to ward off misfortune, are distributed. * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * festival (May 10) * Parishioner Festival (October, on Health and Sports Day) == Grounds == [[File:2013 pp05 Gojoten Jinja Shrine Shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine hall]] * Main Hall - Rebuilt after the {{ill|Kinmon incident|en|Kinmon incident|qid=Q139871|lt=Kinmon Incident|1=Kinmon incident}} in 1864. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * North Gate * East Gate (Main Gate) === Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Tsukushi Tenmangu|ja|Á≠ĎÁīęŚ§©śļÄŚģģ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Tenmangu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504345|1=Tsukushi Tenmangu}} * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine|en|Sarutahiko Shrine|qid=Q11572141|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|1=Sarutahiko Shrine}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|ŚľĀŤ≤°Ś§©|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ŚľĀśČćŚ§©}} Shrine * Consolidated Shrines: {{ill|Shirotayu Shrine|ja|ÁôĹŚ§™Ś§ęÁ•ěÁ§ĺ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotayu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504348|1=Shirotayu Shrine}}, {{ill|ŇĆkuninushi Shrine|ja|Ś§ßŚõĹšłĽÁ•ěÁ§ĺ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ŇĆkuninushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504353|1=ŇĆkuninushi Shrine}}, {{ill|Kompira Shrine|ja|ťáĎŚąÄśĮĒÁĺÖÁ•ěÁ§ĺ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kompira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrines|qid=Q11646488}}, Fuku Shrine 2016 0620 Kyoto Gojo Tenjinsha Nouth Gate.jpg|North Gate and Torii (Matsubara Street) 2016 0620 Kyoto Gojo Tenjinsha Keidaisha.jpg|Sub-shrines behind the main hall 2016 0620 Kyoto Gojo Tenjinsha Tenmangu.jpg|Tsukushi Tenmangu == Nearby == === Tenshi-tsukinuke District === {{seealso|Tenshi-tsukinuke}} The shrine's former expansive grounds (4 towns east-west, 5 towns north-south) were encroached upon during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s urban planning in 1586. The resulting road, cutting through the shrine, gave rise to the name "Tenshi-tsukinuke" (literally "Tenshi Pass-Through").[http://j-town.net/kyoto/news/localtv/193786.html?p=all Kyoto's Most Mysterious Place name: Tenshi-tsukinuke] - J-Town Net Kyoto, October 26, 2014. Variants of the name appear in historical records dating back to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}.[[#27KyotoCityPlacenames|Japan Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Kyoto City]] pp. 930 2016 0620 Kyoto Tenshi-tsukinuke Plate.jpg|Street sign for Tenshi-tsukinuke 2-chome == Access == === By Train === * {{ill|Karasuma Line|en|Karasuma Line|qid=Q762347|lt=Kyoto Municipal Subway Karasuma Line|1=Karasuma Line}} ''' {{ill|GojŇć Station|en|GojŇć Station|qid=Q4980394|lt=GojŇć Station (Kyoto)|1=GojŇć Station}} - About 700 meters northwest ''' {{ill|ShijŇć Station|en|ShijŇć Station|qid=Q2328455|lt=ShijŇć Station (Kyoto)|1=ShijŇć Station}} - About 700 meters southwest * {{ill|Karasuma Station|en|Karasuma Station|qid=Q3969852|lt=Karasuma Station|1=Karasuma Station}} (Hankyu Kyoto Line) - About 800 meters southwest === By Bus === * {{ill|Kyoto City Bus|ja|šļ¨ťÉĹŚłāŚĖ∂„Éź„āĻ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Bus|1=Kyoto City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4858667}} - Alight at "Nishinotoin Matsubara" bus stop == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |language=ja |editor=ŚĻ≥Śá°Á§ĺŚúįśĖĻŤ≥áśĖô„āĽ„É≥„āŅ„Éľ |title=šļ¨ťÉĹ„ÉĽŚĪĪŚüéŚĮļťôĘÁ•ěÁ§ĺŚ§ßšļčŚÖł |date=1997-2-19 |publisher=ŚĻ≥Śá°Á§ĺ |isbn=9784582134018 |ref=kyoto_jinja_daijiten}} * {{Cite book |language=ja |editor=ŚĻ≥Śá°Á§ĺŚúįśĖĻŤ≥áśĖô„āĽ„É≥„āŅ„Éľ |title=śó•śú¨ś≠īŚŹ≤ŚúįŚźćŚ§ßÁ≥ĽÁ¨¨27Ś∑Ľ šļ¨ťÉĹŚłā„ĀģŚúįŚźć |date=1979-9-20 |publisher=ŚĻ≥Śá°Á§ĺ |ref=27_kyoto_city}} * {{Cite book |language=ja |author=Áü≥ÁĒįŚ≠ĚŚĖú |title=šļ¨ťÉĹŚŹ≤Ť∑°šļčŚÖł |date=1994-pp-15 |publisher=śĖįšļļÁČ©ŚĺÄśĚ•Á§ĺ |isbn=440402133X |ref=kyoto_shiseki}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|GojŇć Tenjinsha (TaitŇć-ku)|ja|šļĒśĘĚŚ§©Á•ěÁ§ĺ (ŚŹįśĚĪŚĆļ)|1=Gojo Tenjin Shrine (Tokyo)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GojŇć Tenjinsha (TaitŇć-ku)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojo Tenjin Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} -
